title: Sprint 5 DOM integration tests bypass the production wiring layer entirely and would still pass against a non-functional build
description:
Every Sprint 5 DOM test that purports to verify the in-place editing surface mounts FeedRow.svelte directly with hand-crafted `editingSessionState` and `blockEditorAdapter` props (PROP-FEED-S5-006/007/008/010/011/018/022). None of these tests render the actual production composition (+page.svelte → FeedList → FeedRow). The PROP-FEED-S5-005 channel test imports `editingSessionChannel.ts` directly and asserts the bare module's behavior, not its integration with any consumer.

Because of this, the suite passes (and reports CRIT-201/202/203/206 'PASS') even though FeedList does not subscribe to the channel and +page.svelte does not instantiate the BlockEditorAdapter (see FIND-S5-PHASE3-001). The 'PROP-FEED-S5-018: mount→unmount→remount preserves block-element rendering' test is particularly misleading because the cache continuity it verifies depends on an upstream subscriber that does not exist.

This is a textbook case of integration-blind tests — they validate component-local behavior of FeedRow but say nothing about whether the system as a whole works. The Sprint 5 contract claims this layer is sound; the tests cannot back that claim.

additionalEvidence: promptnotes/src/lib/feed/__tests__/dom/feed-list-editing-channel.dom.vitest.ts:65-100 (imports subscribeEditingSessionState directly; never asserts that any production component invokes it)

promptnotes/src/routes/__tests__/main-route.dom.vitest.ts:181-227 (only checks +page.svelte for absence of forbidden classes; no positive assertion that subscribeEditingSessionState or createBlockEditorAdapter is called)

expectedBehavior: At least one integration test should mount +page.svelte (or FeedList.svelte at minimum), drive a Tauri-event mock that emits `editing_session_state_changed`, and assert that a `data-testid="block-element"` appears inside the row corresponding to the editing noteId. Equivalently, the source-grep gate should assert presence of `subscribeEditingSessionState(` and `createBlockEditorAdapter(` in production source.
actualBehavior: All 'integration' tests bypass the wiring layer; no test fails when FeedList omits the editingSessionChannel subscription or BlockEditorAdapter creation.
